# Hono - Ultrafast Web Framework

Hono is a small, simple, and ultrafast web framework built on Web Standards. The name means "flame" in Japanese. It works on any JavaScript runtime including Cloudflare Workers, Fastly Compute, Deno, Bun, Vercel, Netlify, AWS Lambda, and Node.js. Hono is designed for building Web APIs, edge applications, proxy servers, and full-stack applications with minimal overhead - the `hono/tiny` preset is under 14KB when minified.

The framework provides a delightful developer experience with first-class TypeScript support, clean APIs, and batteries-included middleware. Hono features multiple routers (RegExpRouter for maximum speed, SmartRouter for flexibility) and an RPC system that enables end-to-end type safety between server and client. With built-in middleware for authentication, CORS, caching, logging, and more, Hono lets you "write less, do more" while maintaining excellent performance.

## Quick Start

Create a new Hono project using the starter template.

```bash
npm create hono@latest my-app
cd my-app
npm install
npm run dev
```

## Basic Application

Create a simple Hono application with routes for different HTTP methods.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()

// Basic GET route
app.get('/', (c) => c.text('Hello Hono!'))

// Return JSON response
app.get('/api/users', (c) => {
  return c.json({
    users: [
      { id: 1, name: 'Alice' },
      { id: 2, name: 'Bob' }
    ]
  })
})

// POST with status code
app.post('/api/users', (c) => {
  return c.json({ message: 'User created!' }, 201)
})

// PUT and DELETE
app.put('/api/users/:id', (c) => c.text(`Updated user ${c.req.param('id')}`))
app.delete('/api/users/:id', (c) => c.text(`Deleted user ${c.req.param('id')}`))

export default app
```

## Route Parameters

Handle path parameters, query strings, and wildcards in routes.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()

// Single path parameter
app.get('/users/:id', (c) => {
  const id = c.req.param('id')
  return c.json({ userId: id })
})

// Multiple path parameters
app.get('/posts/:postId/comments/:commentId', (c) => {
  const { postId, commentId } = c.req.param()
  return c.json({ postId, commentId })
})

// Optional parameter
app.get('/api/animal/:type?', (c) => {
  const type = c.req.param('type') || 'unknown'
  return c.text(`Animal type: ${type}`)
})

// Query parameters
app.get('/search', (c) => {
  const query = c.req.query('q')
  const page = c.req.query('page') || '1'
  const tags = c.req.queries('tags') // For multiple values: ?tags=A&tags=B
  return c.json({ query, page, tags })
})

// Regex constraint on parameter
app.get('/posts/:date{[0-9]+}/:title{[a-z]+}', (c) => {
  const { date, title } = c.req.param()
  return c.json({ date, title })
})

// Wildcard route
app.get('/files/*', (c) => c.text('Matched wildcard'))

export default app
```

## Request Handling

Access request headers, body data, and parse different content types.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()

// Access headers
app.get('/info', (c) => {
  const userAgent = c.req.header('User-Agent')
  const contentType = c.req.header('Content-Type')
  return c.json({ userAgent, contentType })
})

// Parse JSON body
app.post('/api/data', async (c) => {
  const body = await c.req.json()
  return c.json({ received: body })
})

// Parse form data
app.post('/submit', async (c) => {
  const body = await c.req.parseBody()
  const name = body['name']
  const email = body['email']
  return c.json({ name, email })
})

// Parse form with file upload
app.post('/upload', async (c) => {
  const body = await c.req.parseBody()
  const file = body['file'] as File
  return c.json({
    filename: file.name,
    size: file.size,
    type: file.type
  })
})

// Access raw request
app.post('/raw', async (c) => {
  const text = await c.req.text()
  const arrayBuffer = await c.req.arrayBuffer()
  return c.text('Received raw data')
})

export default app
```

## Response Methods

Use context methods to return different response types with headers and status codes.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()

// Text response
app.get('/text', (c) => c.text('Plain text response'))

// JSON response
app.get('/json', (c) => c.json({ message: 'Hello', status: 'ok' }))

// HTML response
app.get('/html', (c) => c.html('<h1>Hello HTML!</h1>'))

// Set status code
app.post('/create', (c) => {
  c.status(201)
  return c.json({ created: true })
})

// Set custom headers
app.get('/headers', (c) => {
  c.header('X-Custom-Header', 'CustomValue')
  c.header('Cache-Control', 'max-age=3600')
  return c.text('Check the headers!')
})

// Response with body, status, and headers
app.get('/full', (c) => {
  return c.body('Response body', 200, {
    'X-Custom': 'Header',
    'Content-Type': 'text/plain'
  })
})

// Redirect
app.get('/old-page', (c) => c.redirect('/new-page'))
app.get('/permanent', (c) => c.redirect('/new-location', 301))

// Not found
app.get('/missing', (c) => c.notFound())

export default app
```

## Route Grouping

Organize routes into groups and sub-applications for better code structure.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'

// Create sub-routers
const users = new Hono()
users.get('/', (c) => c.json({ users: [] }))
users.get('/:id', (c) => c.json({ id: c.req.param('id') }))
users.post('/', (c) => c.json({ message: 'User created' }, 201))

const posts = new Hono()
posts.get('/', (c) => c.json({ posts: [] }))
posts.get('/:id', (c) => c.json({ id: c.req.param('id') }))
posts.post('/', (c) => c.json({ message: 'Post created' }, 201))

// Main app
const app = new Hono()

// Mount sub-routers
app.route('/users', users)
app.route('/posts', posts)

// Base path for versioned API
const v1 = new Hono().basePath('/api/v1')
v1.get('/status', (c) => c.json({ version: '1.0' }))

const v2 = new Hono().basePath('/api/v2')
v2.get('/status', (c) => c.json({ version: '2.0' }))

app.route('/', v1)
app.route('/', v2)

// Chained routes
app
  .get('/endpoint', (c) => c.text('GET /endpoint'))
  .post((c) => c.text('POST /endpoint'))
  .delete((c) => c.text('DELETE /endpoint'))

export default app
```

## Middleware

Create and use middleware for request/response processing.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { logger } from 'hono/logger'
import { cors } from 'hono/cors'
import { etag } from 'hono/etag'

const app = new Hono()

// Built-in middleware
app.use('*', logger())
app.use('*', etag())

// CORS middleware with options
app.use('/api/*', cors({
  origin: ['https://example.com', 'https://app.example.com'],
  allowMethods: ['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE'],
  allowHeaders: ['Content-Type', 'Authorization'],
  exposeHeaders: ['X-Custom-Header'],
  maxAge: 600,
  credentials: true
}))

// Custom middleware
const timing = async (c, next) => {
  const start = Date.now()
  await next()
  const ms = Date.now() - start
  c.header('X-Response-Time', `${ms}ms`)
}
app.use('*', timing)

// Middleware that sets variables
app.use('*', async (c, next) => {
  c.set('requestId', crypto.randomUUID())
  await next()
})

// Access variables in handler
app.get('/request-id', (c) => {
  const requestId = c.get('requestId')
  return c.json({ requestId })
})

// Route-specific middleware
const adminOnly = async (c, next) => {
  const token = c.req.header('Authorization')
  if (token !== 'Bearer admin-token') {
    return c.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, 401)
  }
  await next()
}

app.get('/admin', adminOnly, (c) => c.json({ message: 'Admin area' }))

export default app
```

## Basic Authentication

Protect routes with HTTP Basic Authentication middleware.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { basicAuth } from 'hono/basic-auth'

const app = new Hono()

// Basic auth on specific path
app.use('/admin/*', basicAuth({
  username: 'admin',
  password: 'secretpassword'
}))

app.get('/admin/dashboard', (c) => {
  return c.text('Welcome to admin dashboard!')
})

// Multiple users
app.use('/protected/*', basicAuth(
  { username: 'user1', password: 'pass1', realm: 'Protected Area' },
  { username: 'user2', password: 'pass2' }
))

// Custom verification
app.use('/custom/*', basicAuth({
  verifyUser: async (username, password, c) => {
    // Custom logic - e.g., check database
    return username === 'dynamic' && password === 'secret123'
  }
}))

// Auth success callback
app.use('/callback/*', basicAuth({
  username: 'user',
  password: 'pass',
  onAuthSuccess: (c, username) => {
    c.set('authenticatedUser', username)
  }
}))

app.get('/callback/info', (c) => {
  const user = c.get('authenticatedUser')
  return c.json({ user })
})

export default app
```

## Bearer Token Authentication

Authenticate requests using Bearer tokens in the Authorization header.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { bearerAuth } from 'hono/bearer-auth'

const app = new Hono()

// Simple token authentication
app.use('/api/*', bearerAuth({ token: 'my-secret-api-key' }))

app.get('/api/data', (c) => {
  return c.json({ data: 'Protected content' })
})

// Multiple valid tokens
app.use('/multi/*', bearerAuth({
  token: ['token-1', 'token-2', 'token-3']
}))

// Custom token verification
app.use('/custom/*', bearerAuth({
  verifyToken: async (token, c) => {
    // Verify against database or external service
    const validTokens = ['valid-token-1', 'valid-token-2']
    return validTokens.includes(token)
  }
}))

// Role-based access with different tokens
const readToken = 'read-only-token'
const writeToken = 'read-write-token'

app.get('/api/resource', bearerAuth({ token: [readToken, writeToken] }), (c) => {
  return c.json({ message: 'Read access granted' })
})

app.post('/api/resource', bearerAuth({ token: writeToken }), (c) => {
  return c.json({ message: 'Write access granted' })
})

export default app

// Example curl request:
// curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer my-secret-api-key' http://localhost:3000/api/data
```

## JWT Authentication

Verify JSON Web Tokens for secure API authentication.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { jwt } from 'hono/jwt'
import { sign } from 'hono/jwt'
import type { JwtVariables } from 'hono/jwt'

type Variables = JwtVariables

const app = new Hono<{ Variables: Variables }>()

const JWT_SECRET = 'your-secret-key'

// Generate a token (login endpoint)
app.post('/login', async (c) => {
  const { username, password } = await c.req.json()

  // Verify credentials (example)
  if (username === 'user' && password === 'password') {
    const payload = {
      sub: username,
      role: 'admin',
      exp: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + 60 * 60 // 1 hour
    }
    const token = await sign(payload, JWT_SECRET)
    return c.json({ token })
  }

  return c.json({ error: 'Invalid credentials' }, 401)
})

// Protect routes with JWT middleware
app.use('/protected/*', jwt({
  secret: JWT_SECRET,
  alg: 'HS256'
}))

// Access JWT payload in handler
app.get('/protected/profile', (c) => {
  const payload = c.get('jwtPayload')
  return c.json({
    user: payload.sub,
    role: payload.role
  })
})

// JWT from cookie instead of header
app.use('/cookie-auth/*', jwt({
  secret: JWT_SECRET,
  alg: 'HS256',
  cookie: 'auth_token'
}))

// JWT with environment variable
app.use('/env-auth/*', (c, next) => {
  const jwtMiddleware = jwt({
    secret: c.env.JWT_SECRET,
    alg: 'HS256'
  })
  return jwtMiddleware(c, next)
})

export default app
```

## Validation with Zod

Validate request data using Zod schemas for type-safe input handling.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { zValidator } from '@hono/zod-validator'
import { z } from 'zod'

const app = new Hono()

// Define schemas
const createUserSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(2).max(100),
  email: z.string().email(),
  age: z.number().int().min(0).max(150).optional()
})

const querySchema = z.object({
  page: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).default(1),
  limit: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(10),
  search: z.string().optional()
})

// Validate JSON body
app.post('/users', zValidator('json', createUserSchema), (c) => {
  const data = c.req.valid('json')
  // data is typed as { name: string, email: string, age?: number }
  return c.json({ created: data }, 201)
})

// Validate query parameters
app.get('/users', zValidator('query', querySchema), (c) => {
  const { page, limit, search } = c.req.valid('query')
  return c.json({ page, limit, search })
})

// Validate path parameters
const paramSchema = z.object({
  id: z.coerce.number().int().positive()
})

app.get('/users/:id', zValidator('param', paramSchema), (c) => {
  const { id } = c.req.valid('param')
  return c.json({ userId: id })
})

// Validate form data
const formSchema = z.object({
  title: z.string().min(1),
  content: z.string().min(10)
})

app.post('/posts', zValidator('form', formSchema), (c) => {
  const { title, content } = c.req.valid('form')
  return c.json({ title, content }, 201)
})

// Multiple validators
app.put('/users/:id',
  zValidator('param', paramSchema),
  zValidator('json', createUserSchema.partial()),
  (c) => {
    const { id } = c.req.valid('param')
    const updates = c.req.valid('json')
    return c.json({ id, ...updates })
  }
)

export default app
```

## RPC Client

Share API types between server and client for end-to-end type safety.

```ts
// server.ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { zValidator } from '@hono/zod-validator'
import { z } from 'zod'

const app = new Hono()

const route = app
  .get('/posts', (c) => {
    return c.json({
      posts: [
        { id: 1, title: 'Hello World' },
        { id: 2, title: 'Hono is Great' }
      ]
    })
  })
  .post('/posts',
    zValidator('json', z.object({
      title: z.string(),
      body: z.string()
    })),
    (c) => {
      const { title, body } = c.req.valid('json')
      return c.json({ id: 3, title, body }, 201)
    }
  )
  .get('/posts/:id', (c) => {
    const id = c.req.param('id')
    return c.json({ id, title: 'Post Title', body: 'Post content' })
  })

export type AppType = typeof route
export default app

// client.ts
import { hc } from 'hono/client'
import type { AppType } from './server'

const client = hc<AppType>('http://localhost:3000')

// Type-safe API calls
async function main() {
  // GET request
  const postsRes = await client.posts.$get()
  const posts = await postsRes.json()
  console.log(posts.posts) // typed as { id: number, title: string }[]

  // POST request with typed body
  const createRes = await client.posts.$post({
    json: {
      title: 'New Post',
      body: 'This is the content'
    }
  })
  const created = await createRes.json()
  console.log(created.id) // typed as number

  // GET with path parameter
  const postRes = await client.posts[':id'].$get({
    param: { id: '1' }
  })
  const post = await postRes.json()
  console.log(post.title)

  // Get URL without making request
  const url = client.posts.$url()
  console.log(url.pathname) // '/posts'
}
```

## JSX Templates

Render HTML using JSX syntax for server-side rendering.

```tsx
// Configure tsconfig.json:
// { "compilerOptions": { "jsx": "react-jsx", "jsxImportSource": "hono/jsx" } }

import { Hono } from 'hono'
import type { FC } from 'hono/jsx'

const app = new Hono()

// Layout component
const Layout: FC<{ title: string }> = (props) => {
  return (
    <html>
      <head>
        <title>{props.title}</title>
        <meta charset="UTF-8" />
      </head>
      <body>{props.children}</body>
    </html>
  )
}

// Page component with props
const UserList: FC<{ users: { id: number; name: string }[] }> = ({ users }) => {
  return (
    <Layout title="Users">
      <h1>User List</h1>
      <ul>
        {users.map((user) => (
          <li key={user.id}>{user.name}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </Layout>
  )
}

app.get('/users', (c) => {
  const users = [
    { id: 1, name: 'Alice' },
    { id: 2, name: 'Bob' },
    { id: 3, name: 'Charlie' }
  ]
  return c.html(<UserList users={users} />)
})

// Async component
const AsyncData: FC = async () => {
  await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 100))
  return <div>Data loaded!</div>
}

app.get('/async', (c) => {
  return c.html(
    <Layout title="Async">
      <AsyncData />
    </Layout>
  )
})

// Form example
app.get('/form', (c) => {
  return c.html(
    <Layout title="Contact">
      <form method="post" action="/submit">
        <input type="text" name="name" placeholder="Name" />
        <input type="email" name="email" placeholder="Email" />
        <button type="submit">Submit</button>
      </form>
    </Layout>
  )
})

export default app
```

## Cookie Management

Set, get, and delete cookies with the Cookie Helper.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import {
  getCookie,
  setCookie,
  deleteCookie,
  getSignedCookie,
  setSignedCookie
} from 'hono/cookie'

const app = new Hono()

// Set and get cookies
app.get('/cookie', (c) => {
  // Set a cookie
  setCookie(c, 'session', 'abc123', {
    path: '/',
    secure: true,
    httpOnly: true,
    maxAge: 3600, // 1 hour
    sameSite: 'Strict'
  })

  // Get a cookie
  const session = getCookie(c, 'session')

  // Get all cookies
  const allCookies = getCookie(c)

  return c.json({ session, allCookies })
})

// Delete cookie
app.get('/logout', (c) => {
  deleteCookie(c, 'session', {
    path: '/',
    secure: true
  })
  return c.text('Logged out')
})

// Signed cookies for tamper detection
const SECRET = 'my-secret-key'

app.get('/signed', async (c) => {
  // Set signed cookie
  await setSignedCookie(c, 'user_id', '12345', SECRET, {
    path: '/',
    httpOnly: true
  })

  // Get signed cookie (returns false if tampered)
  const userId = await getSignedCookie(c, SECRET, 'user_id')

  if (userId === false) {
    return c.json({ error: 'Cookie tampered' }, 400)
  }

  return c.json({ userId })
})

// Cookie with expiration date
app.get('/remember', (c) => {
  setCookie(c, 'remember_me', 'true', {
    path: '/',
    expires: new Date(Date.now() + 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000), // 30 days
    sameSite: 'Lax'
  })
  return c.text('Remember me set')
})

export default app
```

## Streaming Responses

Stream data to clients using text streams and Server-Sent Events (SSE).

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { stream, streamText, streamSSE } from 'hono/streaming'

const app = new Hono()

// Basic streaming
app.get('/stream', (c) => {
  return stream(c, async (stream) => {
    stream.onAbort(() => {
      console.log('Client disconnected')
    })

    // Write raw bytes
    await stream.write(new Uint8Array([72, 101, 108, 108, 111]))

    // Pipe another stream
    // await stream.pipe(someReadableStream)
  })
})

// Text streaming
app.get('/stream-text', (c) => {
  return streamText(c, async (stream) => {
    await stream.writeln('Starting...')

    for (let i = 1; i <= 5; i++) {
      await stream.sleep(500)
      await stream.writeln(`Processing item ${i}`)
    }

    await stream.write('Done!')
  })
})

// Server-Sent Events
app.get('/sse', (c) => {
  return streamSSE(c, async (stream) => {
    let id = 0

    while (true) {
      const message = `Current time: ${new Date().toISOString()}`

      await stream.writeSSE({
        data: message,
        event: 'time-update',
        id: String(id++)
      })

      await stream.sleep(1000)
    }
  })
})

// SSE with JSON data
app.get('/sse/updates', (c) => {
  return streamSSE(c, async (stream) => {
    const updates = [
      { type: 'user', action: 'joined', name: 'Alice' },
      { type: 'message', content: 'Hello everyone!' },
      { type: 'user', action: 'left', name: 'Bob' }
    ]

    for (const update of updates) {
      await stream.writeSSE({
        data: JSON.stringify(update),
        event: update.type
      })
      await stream.sleep(2000)
    }
  })
})

export default app

// Client-side SSE consumption:
// const eventSource = new EventSource('/sse')
// eventSource.addEventListener('time-update', (e) => console.log(e.data))
```

## Error Handling

Handle errors and customize error responses.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { HTTPException } from 'hono/http-exception'

const app = new Hono()

// Custom 404 handler
app.notFound((c) => {
  return c.json({
    error: 'Not Found',
    message: `Route ${c.req.path} not found`
  }, 404)
})

// Global error handler
app.onError((err, c) => {
  console.error('Error:', err)

  if (err instanceof HTTPException) {
    return err.getResponse()
  }

  return c.json({
    error: 'Internal Server Error',
    message: err.message
  }, 500)
})

// Throw HTTP exceptions
app.get('/protected', (c) => {
  const auth = c.req.header('Authorization')

  if (!auth) {
    throw new HTTPException(401, {
      message: 'Authorization required'
    })
  }

  return c.json({ data: 'secret' })
})

// Custom exception with response
app.get('/custom-error', (c) => {
  const errorResponse = new Response(
    JSON.stringify({ error: 'Custom error', code: 'ERR_001' }),
    { status: 400, headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
  )

  throw new HTTPException(400, { res: errorResponse })
})

// Try-catch in handler
app.get('/safe', async (c) => {
  try {
    const data = await someRiskyOperation()
    return c.json(data)
  } catch (error) {
    return c.json({ error: 'Operation failed' }, 500)
  }
})

export default app
```

## Testing

Test Hono applications using the built-in request helper.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'

const app = new Hono()

app.get('/hello', (c) => c.text('Hello World'))
app.get('/json', (c) => c.json({ message: 'OK' }))
app.post('/users', async (c) => {
  const body = await c.req.json()
  return c.json({ id: 1, ...body }, 201)
})
app.get('/users/:id', (c) => {
  return c.json({ id: c.req.param('id') })
})

// Tests
describe('Hono App', () => {
  it('should return Hello World', async () => {
    const res = await app.request('/hello')
    expect(res.status).toBe(200)
    expect(await res.text()).toBe('Hello World')
  })

  it('should return JSON', async () => {
    const res = await app.request('/json')
    expect(res.status).toBe(200)
    expect(await res.json()).toEqual({ message: 'OK' })
  })

  it('should create user with POST', async () => {
    const res = await app.request('/users', {
      method: 'POST',
      body: JSON.stringify({ name: 'Alice' }),
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }
    })
    expect(res.status).toBe(201)
    expect(await res.json()).toEqual({ id: 1, name: 'Alice' })
  })

  it('should handle path parameters', async () => {
    const res = await app.request('/users/42')
    expect(res.status).toBe(200)
    expect(await res.json()).toEqual({ id: '42' })
  })

  it('should work with Request object', async () => {
    const req = new Request('http://localhost/hello')
    const res = await app.request(req)
    expect(res.status).toBe(200)
  })

  it('should test with mock env', async () => {
    const mockEnv = { API_KEY: 'test-key' }
    const res = await app.request('/hello', {}, mockEnv)
    expect(res.status).toBe(200)
  })
})
```

## Cloudflare Workers Deployment

Deploy a Hono application to Cloudflare Workers with environment bindings.

```ts
// src/index.ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'

type Bindings = {
  MY_KV: KVNamespace
  MY_R2: R2Bucket
  API_SECRET: string
}

const app = new Hono<{ Bindings: Bindings }>()

// Access environment variables
app.get('/config', (c) => {
  const secret = c.env.API_SECRET
  return c.json({ hasSecret: !!secret })
})

// Use KV storage
app.get('/kv/:key', async (c) => {
  const key = c.req.param('key')
  const value = await c.env.MY_KV.get(key)
  return value ? c.text(value) : c.notFound()
})

app.put('/kv/:key', async (c) => {
  const key = c.req.param('key')
  const value = await c.req.text()
  await c.env.MY_KV.put(key, value)
  return c.json({ success: true })
})

// Use R2 storage
app.get('/files/:key', async (c) => {
  const key = c.req.param('key')
  const object = await c.env.MY_R2.get(key)

  if (!object) return c.notFound()

  return new Response(object.body, {
    headers: { 'Content-Type': object.httpMetadata?.contentType || 'application/octet-stream' }
  })
})

app.put('/files/:key', async (c) => {
  const key = c.req.param('key')
  await c.env.MY_R2.put(key, c.req.body)
  return c.json({ uploaded: key })
})

export default app

// wrangler.toml configuration:
// name = "my-worker"
// main = "src/index.ts"
// compatibility_date = "2024-01-01"
//
// [[kv_namespaces]]
// binding = "MY_KV"
// id = "your-kv-namespace-id"
//
// [[r2_buckets]]
// binding = "MY_R2"
// bucket_name = "my-bucket"
//
// [vars]
// API_SECRET = "your-secret"
```

## Bun Deployment

Deploy a Hono application with Bun runtime including static file serving.

```ts
// src/index.ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { serveStatic } from 'hono/bun'
import { logger } from 'hono/logger'

const app = new Hono()

// Middleware
app.use('*', logger())

// Serve static files
app.use('/static/*', serveStatic({ root: './' }))
app.use('/favicon.ico', serveStatic({ path: './public/favicon.ico' }))

// Static with options
app.use('/assets/*', serveStatic({
  root: './public',
  rewriteRequestPath: (path) => path.replace(/^\/assets/, ''),
  onFound: (_path, c) => {
    c.header('Cache-Control', 'public, max-age=31536000')
  },
  onNotFound: (path, c) => {
    console.log(`${path} not found`)
  }
}))

// API routes
app.get('/api/health', (c) => c.json({ status: 'ok' }))

app.get('/api/time', (c) => {
  return c.json({ time: new Date().toISOString() })
})

// Export with custom port
export default {
  port: 3000,
  fetch: app.fetch
}

// Run with: bun run src/index.ts
// Or with hot reload: bun run --hot src/index.ts

// Test file: src/index.test.ts
import { describe, expect, it } from 'bun:test'
import app from './index'

describe('App', () => {
  it('health check', async () => {
    const res = await app.fetch(new Request('http://localhost/api/health'))
    expect(res.status).toBe(200)
    expect(await res.json()).toEqual({ status: 'ok' })
  })
})

// Run tests: bun test
```

## Summary

Hono provides a comprehensive framework for building modern web applications across any JavaScript runtime. Its core strengths include an ultrafast router (RegExpRouter), minimal bundle size, and seamless multi-platform support from edge computing (Cloudflare Workers, Fastly) to server-side runtimes (Node.js, Bun, Deno). The framework excels at building REST APIs, real-time streaming applications, and full-stack web apps with JSX templating.

Key integration patterns include: using middleware chains for authentication (Basic, Bearer, JWT), validation (with Zod or other Standard Schema validators), and cross-cutting concerns (logging, CORS, caching); leveraging the RPC client for end-to-end type safety between server and client; and utilizing platform-specific features like Cloudflare KV/R2 bindings or Bun's static file serving. The framework's adherence to Web Standards ensures consistent behavior across platforms while its TypeScript-first design provides excellent developer experience with full type inference for routes, parameters, and responses.
